prose:
Method provenance
Property: Condensed Fukui indices f⁺ (electrophilic attack), f⁻ (nucleophilic attack), f⁰ (radical attack) and dual descriptor for aniline; most electrophilic and nucleophilic atomic sites identified.  
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP, with RI (density fitting) in PySCF.  
Geometry provenance: fixed input geometry taken from the user-supplied file `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz`; no further optimisation (single-point N, N+1, and N–1 calculations share this geometry).  
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).  
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (0, 1) for the N-electron state; cation doublet (+1, 2) and anion doublet (–1, 2) for the N±1 states used to construct f⁺ and f⁻.  
Software / version: chemkit DFT backend (PySCF; version as bundled with chemkit).  
Convergence & caveats: All three SCF calculations converged; integrity.status = "ok", trustworthy = true. Warnings surfaced verbatim below.  
Live log: the run was launched with
